  "cells": [
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "# In-Silico Aqueous Solubility Prediction (Delaney / ESOL Dataset)\n",
        "\n",
        "**Portfolio project — Cheminformatics & Data Analysis**\n",
        "\n",
        "---\n",
        "\n",
        "### The Science: What is the ESOL Model?\n",
        "\n",
        "Aqueous solubility is one of the first properties checked when evaluating whether a compound could become an oral drug — a molecule that doesn't dissolve well in water will struggle to be absorbed in the body, no matter how good it looks on paper otherwise. Measuring solubility experimentally for every candidate molecule in a screening library is slow and expensive, so cheminformatics offers a shortcut: **predict it computationally from the molecule's structure.**\n",
        "\n",
        "This is exactly what John S. Delaney did in his widely-cited 2004 paper, where he built the **ESOL (Estimated SOLubility)** model. Instead of running a solubility assay in the lab, ESOL estimates it directly from four simple physicochemical descriptors that can be calculated from a molecule's structure alone:\n",
        "\n",
        "$$\\text{Log(Solubility)} = 0.16 - 0.63 \\times \\text{cLogP} - 0.0062 \\times \\text{MW} + 0.066 \\times \\text{RB} - 0.74 \\times \\text{AP}$$\n",
        "\n",
        "Where each term captures a different physical driver of solubility:\n",
        "\n",
        "- **cLogP** (Calculated LogP) — how lipophilic (\"fat-loving\") the molecule is. More lipophilic compounds tend to dissolve worse in water, hence the negative coefficient.\n",
        "- **MW** (Molecular Weight) — larger molecules are generally harder to dissolve.\n",
        "- **RB** (Number of Rotatable Bonds) — molecular flexibility; slightly increases predicted solubility.\n",
        "- **AP** (Aromatic Proportion) — the fraction of the molecule that is aromatic (flat, ring-like structures). More aromatic character usually means poorer solubility.\n",
        "\n",
        "In this notebook, this same logic is rebuilt from scratch using the dataset's own descriptor columns, producing a custom `My_ESOL_Solubility` value for every compound, then this is used to classify each compound and explore which descriptors drive solubility most strongly."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "36xSPlqWpWTs"
      }
    },

        "## Setup"

    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Import the libraries needed for this project.\n",
        "# pandas handles the data itself, matplotlib and seaborn handle all the visualizations.\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "import seaborn as sns\n",
        "\n",
        "# Set a consistent visual style for all plots in this notebook.\n",
        "sns.set_style(\"whitegrid\")"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "MVE7qu9ppp9v"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []

      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 1 — Initial Inspection"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "ggvWkk0Tp4w3"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Before doing anything else, get a quick sense of the dataset's size and structure.\n",
        "print(\"Number of rows:\", df.shape[0])\n",

        "print(\"Shape (rows, columns):\", df.shape)\n",

        "print(df.columns.tolist())"
      ],
      "metadata": {

          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# This cell checks that key columns for the ESOL formula (Calculated_LogP, Molecular Weight, Number of Rotatable Bonds, Aromatic_Proportion) are numeric, preventing potential errors in later calculations.\n",
        "print(df.dtypes)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "lR_Yx_HFqION",
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "collapsed": true,
        "outputId": "9775b2e7-9fa6-4d19-8957-02013d1222a1"
      },

          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# The dataset already contains a ready-made solubility prediction column\n",
        "# ('ESOL predicted log solubility in mols per litre'), calculated using the\n",
        "# original published ESOL formula. This column is dropped here on purpose,\n",
        "# since the goal of this project is to recalculate solubility independently\n",
        "# using our own implementation of the ESOL formula (My_ESOL_Solubility),\n",
        "# not to reuse the dataset's precomputed value.\n",
        "df = df.drop(columns=['ESOL predicted log solubility in mols per litre'])\n",
        "\n",
        "df.head()"
      ],

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 2 — Reorder Columns\n",
        "Two columns are moved into a more logical position:\n",
        "- `smiles` (the compound's structure code) makes more sense sitting right next to `Compound ID`.\n",
        "- `measured log solubility in mols per litre` (the real experimental value) is moved to sit right after `Aromatic_Proportion`, so it will end up next to the newly calculated columns later, making it easy to visually compare measured vs. calculated solubility side by side."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "gV_vKSu00M2p"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Build the new column order as a list, then reindex the DataFrame with it.\n",
        "column_order = df.columns.tolist()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Move 'smiles' to sit directly after 'Compound ID'.\n",
        "column_order.remove('smiles')\n",
        "compound_id_position = column_order.index('Compound ID')\n",
        "column_order.insert(compound_id_position + 1, 'smiles')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Move 'measured log solubility in mols per litre' to sit directly after 'Aromatic_Proportion'.\n",
        "column_order.remove('measured log solubility in mols per litre')\n",
        "aromatic_proportion_position = column_order.index('Aromatic_Proportion')\n",
        "column_order.insert(aromatic_proportion_position + 1, 'measured log solubility in mols per litre')\n",
        "\n",
        "df = df[column_order]\n",
        "\n",
        "# Confirm the new column order visually.\n",
        "df.head()\n"
      ],

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 3 — Missing Values Check"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "lR8yqWT63jUc"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Check every column for missing/NaN values.\n",
        "missing_values = df.isnull().sum()\n",
        "total_missing = missing_values.sum()\n",
        "\n",
        "print(missing_values)\n",
        "\n",
        "if total_missing > 0:\n",
        "  print(\"Missing values found in the following columns:\")\n",
        "  print(missing_values[missing_values > 0])\n",
        "  df = df.dropna()\n",
        "  print()\n",
        "  print(f\"Rows with missing values have been dropped. New shape: {df.shape}\")\n",
        "else:\n",
        "  print(\"No missing or NaN values found in any column. Dataset is clean, no rows dropped.\")\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "QTqmwime3kiq",
        "outputId": "2f87defb-bb69-4d89-b24c-9ee504161bac"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 4 — Calculate My_ESOL_Solubility\n",
        "\n",
        "This step rebuilds Delaney's ESOL formula directly from the dataset's own descriptor columns, producing a custom solubility estimate for each compound:\n",
        "$$\\text{My_ESOL_Solubility} = 0.16 - (0.63 \\times \\text{Calculated_LogP}) - (0.0062 \\times \\text{Molecular Weight}) + (0.066 \\times \\text{Number of Rotatable Bonds}) - (0.74 \\times \\text{Aromatic_Proportion})$$"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "QqOE1HIm388f"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Apply the ESOL formula row by row using pandas operations.\n",
        "df['ESOL_Predicted_LogS'] = (\n",
        "    0.16\n",
        "    - (0.63 * df['Calculated_LogP'])\n",
        "    - (0.0062 * df['Molecular Weight'])\n",
        "    + (0.066 * df['Number of Rotatable Bonds'])\n",
        "    - (0.74 * df['Aromatic_Proportion'])\n",
        " )\n",
        "\n",
        "# Check a random sample of 10 rows to confirm the new column looks reasonable.\n",
        "df[['Compound ID', 'smiles', 'Calculated_LogP', 'Molecular Weight', 'Number of Rotatable Bonds', 'Aromatic_Proportion', 'ESOL_Predicted_LogS']].sample(10)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 469
        },
        "id": "ioGQ6WwR4NZc",
        "outputId": "fea8b19f-8246-4b74-e05c-6d8f0ce6f2f3"
      },

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 5 — Classify Solubility Status\n",
        "\n",
        "Compounds are labeled as **Acceptable** or **Poor** based on a threshold of -4.0 on the `ESOL_Predicted_LogS` scale (compounds with predicted log solubility below -4 are generally considered poorly soluble for oral drug development)."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Z_Bl53IZ4Bae"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Classify each compound using apply() with a lambda function, as required.\n",
        "df['Solubility_Status'] = df['ESOL_Predicted_LogS'].apply(\n",
        "    lambda value: \"Acceptable👍\" if value >= -4.0 else \"Poor👎\"\n",
        "    )\n",
        "\n",
        "# Confirm the new column with a random sample.\n",
        "df[['Compound ID','smiles','ESOL_Predicted_LogS', 'Solubility_Status']].sample(10)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 400
        },

      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 6 — Summary Counts"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Z1Kqq-Z_5uDy"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Count how many compounds fall into each status category.\n",
        "status_counts = df['Solubility_Status'].value_counts()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Get the same breakdown as percentages of the total.\n",
        "status_percentages = df['Solubility_Status'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100\n",
        "\n",
        "# Print a clean summary.\n",
        "for status in status_counts.index:\n",
        "    count = status_counts[status]\n",
        "    percentage = status_percentages[status]\n",
        "    print(f\"{status}: {count} compounds ({percentage:.2f}% of dataset)\")"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "41bMuTSU50lv",
        "outputId": "b49a36c9-b71f-4509-ae0a-fa17a486665c"
      },

          "text": [
            "Acceptable👍: 889 compounds (78.81% of dataset)\n",
            "Poor👎: 239 compounds (21.19% of dataset)\n"
          ]
        }

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Step 7 — Data Visualization\n",
        "\n",
        "Seven plots exploring how each molecular descriptor relates to the calculated solubility, plus one validation plot comparing the calculated values against the real measured values. A shared color palette (blue = Acceptable, red = Poor) is used throughout so status is visually consistent across every plot."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "bMCjOGYG6hZF"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Define a shared color palette and font-size settings so every plot in this notebook looks consistent.\n",
        "# This dictionary is unpacked with ** wherever the same title/label styling is repeated across plots.\n",
        "status_palette = {\"Acceptable👍\": \"#2E86AB\", \"Poor👎\": \"#E63946\"}\n",
        "title_style = {\"fontsize\": 14, \"fontweight\": \"bold\"}\n",
        "label_style = {\"fontsize\": 12}"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "vMzJL3jC6jVO"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### Plot 1 — LogP vs Solubility"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "ZCRUhI246oHV"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))\n",
        "\n",
        "# Ensure the Solubility_Status column is clean by stripping any whitespace\n",
        "df['Solubility_Status'] = df['Solubility_Status'].str.strip()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Plot the two status groups separately so each gets its own color from the palette.\n",
        "for status, color in status_palette.items():\n",
        "    subset = df[df['Solubility_Status'] == status]\n",
        "    ax.scatter(subset['Calculated_LogP'], subset['ESOL_Predicted_LogS'],\n",
        "    alpha=0.5, color=color, label=status)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Add a single regression line fit across all points, in red.\n",
        "sns.regplot(data=df, x='Calculated_LogP', y='ESOL_Predicted_LogS',\n",
        "scatter=False, ax=ax, line_kws={\"color\": \"red\"})\n",
        "\n",
        "ax.set_title(\"Calculated LogP vs. Predicted Solubility\", **title_style)\n",
        "ax.set_xlabel(\"Calculated LogP\", **label_style)\n",
        "ax.set_ylabel(\"ESOL Predicted Log Solubility\", **label_style)\n",
        "ax.legend(title=\"Solubility Status\")\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.savefig('plot1_logp_vs_solubility.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### Plot 7 — Model Validation (Measured vs. Calculated)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "iO9Ldmr5wht5"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))\n",
        "\n",
        "ax.scatter(df['measured log solubility in mols per litre'], df['ESOL_Predicted_LogS'],\n",
        "           alpha=0.5, color=\"#2E86AB\")\n",
        "\n",
        "#Diagonal reference line (y = x): if the calculated values perfectly matched\n",
        "# the measured values, every point would sit exactly on this line.\n",
        "axis_min = min(df['measured log solubility in mols per litre'].min(), df['ESOL_Predicted_LogS'].min())\n",
        "axis_max = max(df['measured log solubility in mols per litre'].max(), df['ESOL_Predicted_LogS'].max())\n",
        "ax.plot([axis_min, axis_max], [axis_min, axis_max], color='red', linestyle='--', label='Perfect Match (y = x)')\n",
        "\n",
        "ax.set_title(\"Measured vs. Calculated Solubility\", **title_style)\n",
        "ax.set_xlabel(\"Measured Log Solubility (mol/L)\", **label_style)\n",
        "ax.set_ylabel(\"My ESOL Solubility (Calculated)\", **label_style)\n",
        "ax.legend()\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.savefig('plot7_model_validation.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 707
        },
        "id": "XnQcfssjwlZO",
